verdict: 2026-07-15 16:23 PDT — Sound and idiomatic. I would build the revised endpoint this way: a thin Express router, bounded global JSON parsing with raw-byte capture, Node crypto for total HMAC verification, a ±300-second freshness window, URL-validation challenge handling, and immediate acknowledgement after the documented no-op purge. The implementation matches Zoom’s current [webhook verification and validation guidance](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/webhooks/), introduces no unnecessary dependency, persistence, network path, or framework abstraction, and cleanly removes the deprecated callback design without residual OAuth coupling. The gate could not be rerun in this read-only review sandbox because Vitest attempted to create `client/node_modules/.vite-temp`; that is an environment limitation, not a design finding.
